overview: Esper Affinity leverages cheap artifact creatures and efficient card advantage to establish early board presence while maintaining interaction. The deck aims to outpace opponents by deploying threats quickly and cycling through its library to find answers. Signature tools like Thoughtcast and Thraben Charm provide the necessary reach and control to close games before opponents can stabilize.
win_conditions: Overwhelming the opponent with a board of cheap artifact creatures
Using card advantage to outvalue midrange and control strategies
Closing out games with efficient removal and direct damage options

keep_criteria: Hands with one or two cheap artifact creatures
Hands containing Thoughtcast or Thraben Charm
Hands with early land drops and artifact synergy
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (prioritize fixing/duals if available), at least one artifact or artifact creature, and a key enabler or interaction (Thoughtcast, Thraben Charm, or Myr Enforcer).
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early plays or artifact synergy
Hands missing removal or card draw
Mulligan on pure land, zero artifacts, or unplayable early turns that lack a way to establish board presence.

early_game: Play lands and cheap artifact creatures to establish board presence
Use early interaction to disrupt opponent's development
Prioritize drawing cards with Thoughtcast to maintain tempo
Turn 1: Play a land, drop a cheap artifact (e.g., Manifold Key, Arcbound Worker, or generic artifact) to build affinity and set up mana/artifact synergy.
Turn 2: Play a second land, deploy Myr Enforcer or another artifact creature.
Use Thoughtcast if you have an instant in the graveyard or can cast one from hand to dig deeper.
Sequence artifacts to maximize affinity discounts on your threats, ensuring you can cast them at reduced costs.
mid_game: Leverage artifact synergies to grow board advantage
Use Thraben Charm to remove key threats or deal direct damage
Maintain card advantage while applying consistent pressure
Preserve mana for interaction until your board is established.
Use Thoughtcast to recycle instants/sorceries from the graveyard, effectively doubling card advantage and digging for key answers.
Avoid overcommitting artifacts if the board is unstable
prioritize creatures with haste, evasion, or enabler synergy.
Commit threats when you have removal ready for their answers or when you can safely swing with pumped Myrs.
Hold back enablers if they can be removed for tempo loss.
late_game: Stabilize with efficient removal and board wipes if necessary
Use card draw to find finishers
Close out games with overwhelming artifact creature boards
Unstoppable artifact swarms powered by Myr Enforcer, backed by recursive Thoughtcast spells and precise Thraben Charm pumps.
Win through combat damage, overwhelming value, or forcing them to trade inefficiently into your interaction.
Go all-in when you have a clear board advantage, their interaction is exhausted, or you can safely swing with pumped Myrs.
Commit when you can protect your attackers with removal, evasion, or when you can close the game before they can stabilize.

key_cards.name: Thoughtcast
key_cards.notes: Use to replace itself and find artifact synergies or removal; prioritize keeping it in hand for late-game value.

key_cards.name: Myr Enforcer
key_cards.notes: Deploy early to trigger artifact enter-the-battlefield effects and apply immediate pressure; pair with other artifacts for maximum impact.

key_cards.name: Thraben Charm
key_cards.notes: Use the damage mode to close games, the removal mode to answer key threats, or the draw mode to maintain card advantage based on game state.

sequencing_tips: Hold up removal for opponent's key threats before committing to board development
Prioritize playing artifact creatures before casting Thoughtcast to maximize board presence
Use Thraben Charm's damage mode when opponent is at low life to secure quick wins
Common mistake: Overcommitting Myr Enforcer without protection, leading to tempo loss.
Always sequence artifacts to maximize affinity discounts.
Thoughtcast synergy relies on graveyard management
avoid milling yourself or discarding your own key instants.
prioritize artifact destruction against artifact-heavy decks, pump in combat, and life gain against aggro.

matchups.advice: Prioritize early removal and board presence to survive the initial onslaught
Apply consistent pressure while using card advantage to outlast their resources
Establish early board presence and use interaction to disrupt their value engines

Use Thoughtcast to cast spells from the graveyard, bypassing counterspells. Sequence artifacts to reduce costs below counter thresholds. Use instant-speed interaction to clear their counterspellers or force them to use resources inefficiently.

Apply early pressure to disrupt their land drops and force trades. Use Thraben Charm to remove key ramp artifacts or pump your threats to race. Do not let them stabilize; trade efficiently and prioritize removing their mana sources.

Win the resource war by prioritizing artifact destruction to disrupt their enablers. Use Thoughtcast to out-recycle and dig for key answers. Track their removal and hold back enablers until they commit. Favor trades that leave you with more artifacts and card advantage.

common_threats: Watch for their removal suite, counterspells, and board wipes.
Note if they are playing around Thraben Charm's artifact destruction mode by protecting key artifacts.
Track graveyard contents for Thoughtcast recursion targets and anticipate when they will attempt to clear your board with sweepers.
